# Questions in Livestream 173

Live Stream #173: I never said she stole my money

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=495" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">8:15</a> | Why are Judges 1:10-15 and Joshua 15:13-19 almost word for word? | He treats Judges as continuing the Joshua narrative, so the repeated episode is not surprising. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=863" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">14:23</a> | Does Deuteronomy 32:8 mean El gave Yahweh Israel, making El the father of Yahweh? | He rejects that reading and stresses the passage's textual complexity without separating Yahweh from the Most High that way. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=1417" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">23:37</a> | Have you ever been an Exodus mythicist? | No. He says the closest he came was simple indifference or agnosticism about the question. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=1530" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">25:30</a> | Doesn't the reference to Pithom in Exodus 1:11 peg the event to Ramesses II? | Yes. He says the site's fortification and repopulation line up with Ramesses II's reign. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=1679" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">27:59</a> | Is the serpent in Genesis 3 a snake? | No. He identifies the serpent as a spiritual being, using serpent language metaphorically rather than describing an ordinary reptile. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=1787" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">29:47</a> | When can biblical time periods, dates, days, and year counts start being taken literally? | He says literal dates can still be hard to reconstruct because calendars changed, and many early year counts remain approximate or idiomatic. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=2238" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">37:18</a> | How did Adam and Eve realize they were naked if clothes were not a thing yet, and why were they ashamed? | He says nakedness symbolizes exposed sin and shame; the issue is not clothing itself but trying to cover sin. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=2408" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">40:08</a> | How should God walking in the garden in Genesis 3:8 be explained to a child? | He says the text shows God present in sacred space, drawing Adam and Eve out of hiding after their sin. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=2715" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">45:15</a> | Why haven't you written a Sunday School curriculum? | He says he once proposed one, but the church wanted lighter material rather than a serious educational curriculum. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=3021" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">50:21</a> | Is there an Enoch-like figure at number seven in Egyptian king or god lists? | No. He says Egyptian king-and-god lists do not preserve a direct parallel of that kind. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=3255" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">54:15</a> | Why does the Old Testament forbid eating leavened bread during Passover? | He says yeast is associated with mold and decay, so removing leaven fits Passover purity and cleansing rather than a blanket rejection of yeast. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=3761" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:02:41</a> | Why did John choose conquest, war, famine, and death as riders in Revelation? | He connects the four horsemen to Zechariah 6 and says John is describing agents of divine wrath and judgment. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=4065" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:07:45</a> | Is it really true that we do not know how the pyramids were built? | He says we know the broad method fairly well from quarries, transport evidence, unfinished monuments, and construction remains. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=4196" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:09:56</a> | Did building pyramids have an economic impact, and is there documentation for that? | He says yes, citing traditions such as Papyrus Westcar that remember major pyramid builders as economically burdensome rulers. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=4380" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:13:00</a> | Why do people use the pyramids as an argument against trusting the Bible or believing in God? | He says that argument misunderstands both Egyptian history and the Bible, since the Bible does not claim Israelites built the pyramids. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=4606" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:16:46</a> | Does Mark 4:35-40 connect to Psalm 3 or Psalm 4:8? | He says either psalm could be applied secondarily, but Mark does not make an explicit quotation or direct connection. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=4802" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:20:02</a> | Are there any non-Christian sources for Herod's massacre, or even general outside evidence? | He says there is no direct outside source and that ancient historians may have regarded it as too minor to record. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=4935" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:22:15</a> | Does Deuteronomy 3:4-5 fit a late thirteenth-century BC conquest if many Canaanite cities were unwalled? | He says yes, because the text itself distinguishes fortified cities from many unwalled towns. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=5392" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:29:52</a> | Have you read Paul and the Resurrection of Israel? | No. He says he does not usually comment on books he has not read. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=5409" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:30:09</a> | What do you wish more people knew about the biblical feasts? | He highlights that the ban on boiling a kid in its mother's milk is a feast-related prohibition against a pagan ritual, not a general cheeseburger ban. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=5915" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:38:35</a> | Can you give sources for reading on boiling a kid in its mother's milk? | He says he found it in a commentary and connects it to Ugaritic texts, but he cannot name the specific source from memory. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=6084" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:41:24</a> | What is the best case that Deuteronomy resembles first-millennium treaties more than second-millennium treaties? | He says he does not think there is a strong case and would defend a late Bronze Age core for Deuteronomy on other grounds. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=6186" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:43:06</a> | Have we elevated the law to be more than intended? | He says yes, if the law is being read as prescriptive modern law rather than ancient Near Eastern case law. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=6504" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:48:24</a> | Why did Jesus get baptized in Matthew 3:15? | He says Jesus' baptism fulfilled righteousness and marked him for priestly service, not repentance from sin. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=6894" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:54:54</a> | Is it true that in the ancient Near East a year was 360 days? | He says it depends on the calendar; some civil calendars could be 360 days, while Egyptian, lunar, and lunisolar calendars differed. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=7070" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:57:50</a> | What should be said to people who claim John 6:51-58 is about the Eucharist? | He says the passage is primarily about Jesus as the living bread and manna fulfillment, not directly about the Eucharist. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=7494" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:04:54</a> | Why does the early church claim Joachim is Mary's father when the Bible says Eli? | He says that claim likely comes from the Protoevangelium of James, an apocryphal source he regards as unreliable. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=7688" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:08:08</a> | How is Old Arabic linguistically different from Quranic and modern Arabic? | He says he is not an Arabic specialist and can only speak generally about Old Arabic's Semitic and alphabetic features. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=7791" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:09:51</a> | What does the Spirit do in baptism according to Scripture? | He says he does not have a firm view because the text is vague; he distinguishes regeneration and baptism as a pledge of new life. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=7993" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:13:13</a> | Does turning the other cheek mean not reacting to insults, or asking to be treated as an equal rather than a slave? | He says it means not repaying insult with evil, not a request for equal social treatment. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=8265" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:17:45</a> | Why do Christians keep making bad films? | He blames lazy writing and urges Christians in the arts to learn their craft and make excellent work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=9323" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:35:23</a> | As an evidentialist, how are you still a Christian? | He says he remains Christian because he is an evidentialist and thinks online claims that there is no evidence are ignorant. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=9411" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:36:51</a> | What is the best documented case for a Eucharistic miracle? | He says he does not track Eucharistic miracle claims and prefers to ground his faith in Scripture rather than secondary miracle reports. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=9566" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:39:26</a> | Are Luke 1:6 and Romans 10:13 contradictory? | He says Romans 10:13 does not seem connected; if the intended text is Romans 3:10, the passages use righteousness in different senses. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=10194" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:49:54</a> | How does transgression differ from abomination and sin? | He distinguishes abomination on a holiness/cleanness spectrum from sin categories such as iniquity, transgression, and rebellion. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=10552" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:55:52</a> | Were there gangs in ancient Egypt, and is there evidence of gang culture? | He says gangs could appear when civil order broke down, but he does not describe Egypt as having a persistent gang culture. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=10640" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:57:20</a> | What do you think about reading the Genesis 5 genealogy as a prophecy of Jesus? | He rejects it as thoroughly debunked and says it relies on speculative name meanings and hidden-message exegesis. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=10841" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:00:41</a> | What are the main problems with Protestantism? | He declines the question as too broad and divisive, saying he would address specific theological issues instead. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=10978" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:02:58</a> | Do you think human beings existed before Adam and Eve? | He says the text may allow that possibility, with Adam and Eve serving as specially appointed representatives of humanity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=11275" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:07:55</a> | What do Leviathan, Behemoth, Rahab, and similar figures in Job represent? | He identifies them as chaos monsters or spirit beings associated with creation and later replaced by the divine council's role. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=11440" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:10:40</a> | Do people who are brain dead still have their soul? | He says he is not qualified to answer definitively; a bioethicist in the room says many would answer yes because they are not fully dead. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=11559" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:12:39</a> | If the Shroud is not Jesus' burial cloth, was it a painting of him? | He says the cloth does not date to Jesus and may be a painting, but he withholds fuller claims pending experimental work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=11678" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:14:38</a> | What role should tradition play in theology? | He says tradition can guide theology, but its value depends on whether it grows from exegesis or from later cultural assumptions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=11784" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:16:24</a> | Who is your favorite early church figure, and why? | He does not choose one favorite, but names figures such as Clement, Chrysostom, Ambrose, Irenaeus, and Theophilus of Antioch. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=11886" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:18:06</a> | Where was the Garden of Eden located? | He says we do not know for sure, but he would guess near Sealand, much of which is now underwater. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=12018" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:20:18</a> | What kind of knowledge of evil do divine beings or angels have? | He says holy angels have intellectual knowledge of evil, while fallen beings have experiential knowledge of evil. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=12194" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:23:14</a> | Is the Septuagint addition about Pharaoh conquering Gezer in Joshua 16 likely the Merneptah campaign? | No. He identifies it as a scribal gloss about Gezer connected with Solomon, not the Merneptah campaign. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=12377" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:26:17</a> | How should political voting be understood biblically or morally, especially with 1 Corinthians 5:1-5? | He says voting is a civic duty and 1 Corinthians 5 addresses church discipline, so voters must weigh candidates by conscience and context. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=12932" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:35:32</a> | Were there political philosophy texts in ancient Egypt meant to educate and guide rulers? | He says none are known; rulers were trained in royal settings, and Egypt was not a political-philosophy culture in that sense. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=12990" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:36:30</a> | What ancient texts are you sad we lost? | He names the original Book of Jashar and Egyptian court daybooks, especially for important reigns such as the Hyksos period. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=13328" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:42:08</a> | What is the best argument that Jewish traditions borrowed from Zoroastrianism in exile, and why is it wrong? | He says he is not qualified to answer because Zoroastrianism and Persian-period Jewish literature are outside his specialization. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=13430" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:43:50</a> | Is Leah a foil to a bovine goddess? | He initially does not understand the question; after clarification, he says the weak-eyed/Hathor connection is too oblique to support the claim. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=13540" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:45:40</a> | What should a scholar do if they repeatedly get the scholarship on an issue wrong? | He says scholars should do due diligence, admit mistakes, learn, and correct themselves rather than be automatically excluded from discussion. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=13844" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:50:44</a> | What is your opinion of the latest Shroud evidence and new dating information? | He says not to pin hopes on it, notes serious reservations, and says a fuller video is planned. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=13940" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:52:20</a> | Did you see Dan's response to Michael Jones? | He says yes, and also saw Michael Jones's response, but gives only a brief critical reaction. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=14090" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:54:50</a> | Was Abraham Syrian? | He says Abraham was northern Mesopotamian and Amorite, not Syrian, though Amorites originated in the northern Levant. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=14224" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:57:04</a> | If an ancient Mesopotamian mentioned god without specificity, might they be invoking a vague all-powerful deity? | He says it could depend on Mesopotamian context, but Egypt provides a counterexample where an unspecified god often means a local or highest deity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/80oRlFB_V60?t=14371" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:59:31</a> | What do you think of historiography, or history through narrative? | He distinguishes that from a narrative fallacy and says narrative forms can still do real historical work. |
